results.possibleIdentity: Russell Dian is a photographer whose career spans from the late 1960s to the present day. He was notably active during the late 1960s and early 1970s, a period when he was associated with the influential video art and technology collective Raindance Corporation. He contributed photography to their landmark journal, "Radical Software," which was published between 1970 and 1974 and served as a major forum for the integration of art, engineering, and social change. His work in this era aligns with the activities of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, as both entities shared a mission to foster collaborations between artists and scientists. Professionally, Dian has also served as a photographer and series editor for various scientific and educational publications, including the Harvard Project Physics and textbooks for Holt Science. His credits include photographs for children's literature, such as the "Slim Goodbody" series by John Burstein and "A Man Can Be..." (1981). He remains active in the arts community; in 2018, he participated in the mixed-media exhibition "What's the Matter?" at the Atlantic Highlands Arts Council. He is married to Alicia Benoist, an arts development executive, and the couple is known as patrons of several New York musical organizations, including the Orchestra of St. Luke's and the American Symphony Orchestra.
